competitors.name: S&P 500
competitors.rules: Buys SPY with the whole book on the first forward session and holds — the market baseline.

trade_log.reason: buy & hold: all-in on day one

competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

analyst.generated_by: financial-analyst skill (Claude for Financial Services equity-research methodology)
analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
analyst.sizing: GE to 0.15 of equity as the new quality-momentum anchor; fund it by exiting HOOD and trimming the high-beta sleeve. Hold MU as a moderate semis anchor (not adding), keep MRNA, add value (TGT, BAC). ~42% cash retained.

regime.label: Hawkish Fed, late-cycle pullback within an uptrend — value/quality over long-duration growth
regime.note: FOMC held at 3.50-3.75% on 6/17 but REMOVED its prior cut outlook and raised 2026 inflation projections (3.6% headline); under new Chair Warsh the market now prices a possible HIKE as early as October, with Deutsche Bank looking for two hikes. SPY is -2.3% over the last 5d/20d (a repricing of long-duration growth) but still +15.6% over 60d, and stabilized flat on 6/24. Higher-for-longer rates penalize unprofitable, high-multiple software/AI most; reward earnings-backed value, banks (NII) and rate-insulated quality.

analyst.thesis: What changed since last tick: the macro turned overtly hostile and my book's flaw got exposed. The Fed dropped its cut bias on 6/17 and the tape spent the week derating long-duration growth; meanwhile my 'stabilizer' HOOD cracked another -5.8% on 6/24, proving it was never ballast — it's a 50x-P/E high-beta brokerage. So this tick rotates from a high-multiple, rate-sensitive tilt into a diversified value/quality book that actually fits a hawkish regime, while keeping a large cash buffer. The new anchor is GE (Aerospace) — at 52-wk highs on 6/24, real earnings, aero/defense demand insulated from rate-driven derating. I add value that gets paid in a higher-for-longer world: TGT (18x P/E, 3.4% yield, breaking to highs on a turnaround) and BAC (14x, 1.5x book, NII beneficiary, at highs). I keep MU as a moderate semis anchor on its through-close merits (pulled back ~13% from the 6/22 high into the print, +193% over 60d, HBM franchise intact) and keep MRNA as an idiosyncratic, non-rate-driven biotech diversifier. INTEGRITY NOTE: Micron reported after the 6/24 close and popped ~9% after-hours on a strong HBM print — but the paper engine fills this rebalance at the 6/24 regular-session close (pre-pop), and every other competitor only sees price through that close. Sizing MU up on the after-hours reaction would be trading on information my fill price doesn't reflect, i.e. look-ahead. So I deliberately do NOT chase the print; MU is a hold, not an add, and the settled reaction is next tick's trade. Net: one semis name instead of three correlated/leveraged ones, four uncorrelated sleeves, ~42% cash.

evidence.point: Fed held 3.50-3.75% on 6/17 but removed its cut outlook and raised 2026 inflation forecasts; market now prices a possible hike by October — a direct headwind for high-multiple, long-duration growth and a tailwind for value/banks.

GE Aerospace closed at a fresh 52-wk high of 365.62 on 6/24 (+2.6% on the day, only ~1% off its intraday high), RS +18.6 vs SPY over 20d; aerospace/defense earnings are insulated from rate-driven multiple compression. P/E 43.9, div 0.43%.

TGT trades at 17.7x P/E with a 3.4% dividend yield and closed at a 52-wk high (141.19, +5.3% on 6/24) — cheap, defensive retail turnaround, the lowest-multiple/lowest-correlation sleeve in the book and rate-resilient.
BAC at 14.4x P/E and 1.5x book, 1.9% yield, closed at a 52-wk high (57.75) on 6/24; major banks are direct beneficiaries of higher-for-longer rates via net interest income.

MU pulled back ~13% from its 6/22 52-wk high (1213.56) into the 6/24 print, but is still +17% over 20d and +193% over 60d with HBM capacity committed through CY2026 — franchise intact; held moderate, not chased on the after-hours reaction.
HOOD fell another -5.8% on 6/24 (now -10% from my 6/18 entry) — a 50x-P/E high-beta name, not the 'stabilizer' I treated it as; exited to cut book beta in a hostile tape.

risks: If the Fed's hawkish turn proves overdone and rates ease, the high-multiple growth/semis I just trimmed (MU, the AI complex) re-rate fastest and this value/quality tilt lags a risk-on bounce.
GE at a 52-wk high and 44x P/E has little valuation cushion — any aero/defense order or guidance disappointment hits a crowded momentum name hard.
MU's strong after-hours print (which I am not trading) gaps the 6/25 open higher, so by refusing to chase I forgo upside if the reaction sticks — a deliberate integrity cost.
TGT/BAC 'value' can stay cheap (value traps) if consumer/credit conditions deteriorate into a higher-for-longer slowdown.
Carrying ~42% cash caps upside if the tape rips; chosen on purpose given a -10.6% drawdown and a hawkish regime.

data_examined.label: Momentum/RS screen over all 100 universe names (1d/5d/20d/60d returns, RS vs SPY, distance from 60d high) on the fresh 2026-06-24 snapshot
Robinhood fundamentals (P/E, P/B, 52-wk range, yield, market cap) for MU, MRVL, MRNA, GE, HOOD, TGT, DE, AXP, BAC, SNOW
Fed June 2026 decision and rate-path outlook
Micron fiscal Q3 2026 earnings (reported after 6/24 close) — read for catalyst context, not traded
Prior tick book, realized P&L and reflection

rationale.GE: New top-conviction anchor and the diversifier the book lacked. GE Aerospace closed at a fresh 52-wk high on 6/24 (+2.6% on the day) with RS +18.6 vs SPY over 20d, yet it's a real-earnings aero/defense franchise whose backlog is insulated from the rate-driven multiple compression hammering long-duration growth. It's the kind of quality-momentum name a hawkish regime rewards, and it has near-zero correlation to my semis/biotech sleeves. Sized to 0.15 as the largest single position.
rationale.MU: Held as a moderate semis anchor, NOT added. The franchise is intact — HBM committed through CY2026, +193% over 60d — and the ~13% pullback from the 6/22 high into the print improved the entry. But it's a 50x-P/E, rate-sensitive name in a hawkish tape, so I keep it modest. Critically: MU printed bullish after the 6/24 close and popped ~9% after-hours, but this rebalance fills at the pre-pop 6/24 close while every other competitor only sees price through that close — sizing up on the after-hours reaction would be look-ahead, so I refuse to chase it and let next tick trade the settled move.
rationale.TGT: The value/defensive sleeve. 17.7x P/E and a 3.4% yield with the stock breaking to a 52-wk high on a turnaround (+5.3% on 6/24) — cheap, low-multiple, consumer-staple-ish retail that holds up when rates stay high and growth derates. Lowest correlation to the rest of the book; sized 0.12.
rationale.BAC: Direct hawkish-regime beneficiary. At 14.4x P/E and 1.5x book with a 1.9% yield, a major bank that earns more net interest income the longer rates stay elevated; closed at a 52-wk high on 6/24. The cheapest name in the book and a clean value tilt; sized 0.10.
rationale.MRNA: Kept as the idiosyncratic diversifier. Biotech driven by pipeline/clinical catalysts rather than the rate cycle, so it's uncorrelated to the macro headwind hitting everything else; +28% over 20d and holding near its entry. Small at 0.08 given it's unprofitable (P/E -7.5).

reflection.looking_back: Going into 6/24 I held a de-risked book — HOOD 0.20, MU 0.15, MRNA 0.10, ~55% cash — after cutting the leveraged/high-beta semis (ARM, SOXL) and halving MU on 6/23. On 6/24 the book gave back a little more: HOOD -5.8%, MU -0.3% on the regular session, MRNA -1.0%, leaving equity ~$89.4 (-10.6% all-time). Most of the drawdown was the 6/23 semis crack on the 6/18 entries; the de-risking limited the 6/24 bleed but didn't reverse it.
worked: Exiting the leveraged/high-beta semis ahead of the print was right — ARM kept sliding (-9.4% over 5d) and a 3x SOXL into a cracking tape was the exact risk the plan flagged.
Raising cash to ~55% preserved dry powder, so the 6/24 drift cost little and leaves room to redeploy into the value/quality rotation now.
missed: Calling HOOD the book's 'stabilizer' was wrong — it's a 50x-P/E high-beta brokerage and was the single biggest drag on 6/24 (-5.8%); real ballast should have been a low-beta quality/value name, not HOOD.

I kept the book tilted to high-multiple, rate-sensitive names (MU 50x, HOOD 50x) even though the Fed had turned overtly hawkish on 6/17 — the macro argued for a value/quality tilt a week before I made it.
reflection.adjustment: Rotate the high-beta sleeve into lower-beta quality/value that fits a hawkish regime: cut HOOD entirely, anchor on GE (aero/defense at 52-wk highs), add value (TGT 18x, BAC 14x), hold a moderate MU and the idiosyncratic MRNA, and keep ~42% cash.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2025-11-03–2026-06-24, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-ranks its best ideas seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). The panel's rank-weighted (Borda) consensus becomes a multi-name book (top 5 names, weight ∝ points). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
